topic.title: HTML Table Structure and Spans
topic.description: HTML tables present tabular data using rows, columns, headers, and spans for semantic structure.

content.text: Building Accessible and Semantic HTML Tables

text.value: An HTML table is built from a hierarchy of elements. The
element is the outer container. Inside it,
element provides an accessible title for the table, and the
cells tells assistive technologies whether a header applies to a column, row, or group of cells.

rows: Outer container for all table content
Accessible title describing the table's purpose
Groups header rows at the top of the table
Groups the primary data rows of the table
Groups summary or footer rows at the bottom
Defines a single row of cells
Header cell, bold by default, exposes column or row label to screen readers
Declares whether a th applies to a col, row, colgroup, or rowgroup
Merges a cell across multiple columns
Merges a cell across multiple rows

content.title: Tables Are for Tabular Data Only
content.text: Never use table elements to control page layout. Layout tables were a pre-CSS pattern that breaks screen readers, prevents responsive design, and violates WCAG 1.3.1. Use CSS grid or flexbox for layout instead.

examples.title: Complete Accessible Table with thead, tbody, tfoot, and caption
examples.description: A fully structured table showing all major elements in their correct positions, with scope attributes on header cells and a caption providing an accessible title for screen reader users.
examples.code_snippet:
Q1 2024 Regional Sales Summary
Region Jan Feb Mar Total
North $42,000 $38,500 $51,200 $131,700
South $29,800 $33,100 $27,600 $90,500
East $55,400 $61,200 $58,900 $175,500
West $47,300 $44,800 $49,100 $141,200
All Regions $174,500 $177,600 $186,800 $538,900
examples.output: A styled table renders with a bold left-aligned caption above it. The header row has a dark navy background with white text listing Region, Jan, Feb, Mar, and Total. Four data rows follow with alternating white and light grey backgrounds, each starting with a bold row header (North, South, East, West) followed by dollar figures. The totals row at the bottom has a light grey background with bold text. Screen readers announce the caption before the data, and each cell is announced with its column and row header context.

examples.title: WRONG vs RIGHT: Layout Table vs Semantic Table
examples.description: Contrasts a table misused for two-column page layout against a properly structured data table, showing exactly what breaks for accessibility and responsive design when tables are used for layout.
examples.code_snippet:
...
...
...
...
Product Comparison
Feature Free Plan Pro Plan
Storage 5 GB 100 GB
Users 1 Unlimited
examples.output: The WRONG layout table renders the navigation and main content side by side but announces column counts and cell positions to screen readers, confusing users who expect landmark navigation. On narrow screens it does not reflow. The RIGHT version uses a CSS grid div for layout and a properly captioned, scoped table for the product comparison data, which screen readers navigate correctly with full header context for every cell.

examples.title: colspan and rowspan for Complex Data Grids
examples.description: Shows colspan used to create a grouped column header that spans multiple columns, and rowspan used to merge a row header across multiple rows, a common pattern in financial and scheduling tables.
examples.code_snippet:
Employee Schedule: Week of 15 Jan 2024
Employee Morning Afternoon
9:00 AM 11:00 AM 1:00 PM 3:00 PM
Alice Design Review Team Standup Client Call Free
Bob Free Team Standup Free Sprint Planning
Pair Programming Architecture Code Review Testing Free
Documentation Free Deployment Retrospective
examples.output: The table renders with a two-row header. The top header row shows 'Employee' spanning both rows, then 'Morning' spanning two columns and 'Afternoon' spanning two columns in a darker navy. The second header row fills in the four time slots underneath. In the body, the 'Pair Programming' row header merges across two rows. Screen readers announce each cell with its full header chain, for example 'Afternoon, 3:00 PM, Pair Programming: Retrospective'.

text.value: when presenting data that has a genuine relationship between rows and columns, such as schedules, price comparisons, financial reports, or sports statistics.
when data cells or headers logically span multiple columns or rows in the real-world data, such as grouped column headers or merged category labels.
on every table so screen reader users hear a description of the table before navigating its cells, satisfying the
instead, as layout tables break screen reader navigation and prevent responsive reflow.
when the same data could be represented as multiple simpler tables, as deeply merged cells create confusing navigation paths for screen reader users.
, screen readers must guess whether a header applies to a column, row, or group, often getting it wrong.
, screen readers cannot distinguish header rows from data rows, forcing users to mentally track which row contains column labels across a large table.
, grouped data relationships that span multiple cells must be duplicated across individual cells, creating visual and semantic redundancy.
attributes, screen readers read tables as grids of isolated values with no way to understand what each cell represents in relation to its headers.
An HTML table is like a spreadsheet with labelled axes.
is the frozen top row with column labels,
is the scrollable data region, and
is the frozen totals row at the bottom. The
attribute tells assistive technology which axis each label belongs to.

as the merge cells function in Excel. When two or more adjacent cells share the same label or value, you merge them into one cell that physically spans the space of the originals, rather than repeating the label in every cell.
element is the table's headline. Just as a chart in a report has a figure title below it explaining what the chart shows,
gives screen reader users a meaningful description before they begin navigating the cells, preventing the disorientation of landing inside a grid with no context.
cells: screen readers guess header direction incorrectly, breaking cell announcements in complex tables.
cells in spanned rows shifts all subsequent columns, breaking the grid alignment.
for row headers: screen readers treat them as plain data, so row context is lost when navigating columns.
cells to keep column headers visible as users scroll. This works because
is a distinct section that browsers can pin independently of
or axe DevTools to catch missing
cells, and incorrect cell counts caused by miscalculated

subtopics.title: Table Structure Elements (thead, tbody, tfoot)
subtopics.description: The thead, tbody, and tfoot elements group a table's rows into header, body, and footer sections.

text.value: Row group elements give a table semantic structure beyond individual rows.
wraps the primary data rows, and
wraps summary or closing rows such as totals. All three are optional children of
is implicitly created by browsers even when omitted. A table may contain multiple
elements to segment large datasets into logical groups. The
attribute so assistive technologies can associate header labels with their data cells.

content.title: tfoot can appear before tbody in source order
content.text: The HTML spec allows tfoot to be written before tbody in the source. Browsers render it at the bottom regardless of source order, and when printing a long table across multiple pages, the tfoot content repeats at the bottom of each printed page automatically.

examples.title: Table with Multiple tbody Sections for Grouped Data
examples.description: Demonstrates using multiple tbody elements to divide a large dataset into named sections, with a colgroup used to apply column-level styling, and a tfoot for grand totals.
examples.code_snippet:
Annual Budget by Department
Department Q1 Q2 Total
Engineering
Frontend $80,000 $85,000 $165,000
Backend $95,000 $98,000 $193,000
Marketing
Content $40,000 $42,000 $82,000
Paid Ads $60,000 $55,000 $115,000
Grand Total $275,000 $280,000 $555,000
examples.output: The table shows a dark header row with four column labels. Below it, two distinct data groups appear separated by full-width blue section labels 'Engineering' and 'Marketing', each followed by their department rows with dollar figures. The Total column has a subtle background tint from the colgroup styling. A bold grey totals row closes the table at the bottom. Screen readers read each section header before the rows within it, and the grand total is announced last.

text.value: like the three zones of a printed report: the title and column headers at the top, the rows of data in the middle, and the signature and totals block at the bottom. Each zone has a different visual weight and a different semantic role, and printers know to repeat the top and bottom zones on every page.
elements inside one table are like dividers in a binder. All the pages belong to the same binder (
), but the dividers visually and semantically separate Engineering from Marketing from Sales, making the data scannable without splitting it into separate tables.
element itself) for sticky column headers on scrollable tables. Applying sticky to the
row group element rather than its cells is a common mistake that does not work in most browsers.

subtopics.title: Table Span Attributes (colspan, rowspan)
subtopics.description: The colspan and rowspan attributes merge table cells across columns or rows for grouped layouts.

text.value: cell specifies how many columns that cell should occupy. The
attribute specifies how many rows it should span downward. Both accept integer values of 1 or greater. When a cell spans multiple columns with
cells in that row must be omitted from the markup because the spanned cell already occupies that space. The same applies to rows below a cell with
elements that span multiple columns or rows, use
to correctly associate the header with its group of cells.

content.title: Cell count must remain consistent across every row
content.text: After applying colspan and rowspan, every row in the table must resolve to the same total number of columns when spanned cells are counted. A row with fewer cells than expected causes the browser to render a broken grid that is nearly impossible to fix without restarting the cell count from scratch.

examples.title: Pricing Table Using colspan and rowspan
examples.description: A real-world pricing table where a 'Most Popular' badge spans multiple rows using rowspan, and a 'All Plans Include' footer row uses colspan to span the full table width, demonstrating both attributes together.
examples.code_snippet:
Plan Comparison
Feature Starter Pro Enterprise
Price / mo Free $12 Most Popular $49
Storage 5 GB 50 GB 500 GB
Users 1 5 Unlimited
Support Community Email 24/7 Phone
All plans include SSL, 99.9% uptime SLA, and free data migration.
examples.output: The table shows a header row with Starter, Pro (spanning two columns including the badge area), and Enterprise. Four feature rows follow. The Pro column has a blue vertical 'Most Popular' badge cell that spans all four feature rows. The table closes with a full-width light blue italic note spanning all five columns. Screen readers announce the Pro column header with its colgroup scope for each merged cell, and the badge is read once at the start of its rowspan.

text.value: like the merge cells button in Excel or Google Sheets. When you merge a 2x1 range horizontally, you get one wide cell that counts as two. You cannot add another cell in that space, and the spreadsheet automatically hides the ghost cells. HTML works the same way: you omit the cells that the span covers.
group headers is like a time table with 'Morning' printed over two time slots. The word 'Morning' acts as a parent label for 9:00 AM and 11:00 AM. Using
on that header cell tells screen readers to associate it with all the columns it spans, not just the first one.
When debugging a broken table grid caused by incorrect
. This makes every cell boundary visible and reveals which rows have too many or too few cells at a glance.

seo_metadata.meta_title: HTML Table Structure and Spans | thead, tbody, colspan, rowspan Guide
seo_metadata.meta_description: Learn how to build accessible HTML tables using thead, tbody, tfoot, caption, scope, colspan, and rowspan for correct screen reader support and semantic markup.
